title: New Zealand’s 700 MHz spectrum auction defined the 4G landscape
tags: The 700 MHz band is the "prime real estate";the 'Big Three' still controlling 97.5 percent of the market;Spark's purchase of 2300 MHz spectrum from Craig Wireless and Whoosh shifted the broadband competitive balance again;government fast-tracked 5G spectrum allocation;Vodafone stole a march on competition rolling out 4G in Auckland early in 2013
